methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Series B → next-stage step-up multiplier (1.9x) to $10.0B last post money.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 5 same-sector + same-stage peers in Robotics & Manufacturing. Range: $300M - $1.8B.

private.name: Bedrock Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.75B

private.name: Dexterity
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $1.65B

private.name: Collaborative Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $500M+

private.name: Fairmat

private.name: GrayMatter Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), same stage (Series B), post-money $300M+

data.narrative:
1X Technologies is a Series B robotics and manufacturing company founded by Bernt Øivind Børnich, currently valued at $10.0B post-money on $123.5M raised to date. The peer universe comprises 9 private robotics firms and 1 public comparable, with the primary Stage B cohort including Bedrock Robotics ($1.8B), Dexterity ($1.6B), Collaborative Robotics ($500M), Fairmat ($350M), and GrayMatter Robotics ($300M).

Two valuation methods anchor this analysis. Stage-progression modeling applies a typical Series B step-up multiplier of 1.9x to the $10.0B last post-money valuation, implying $19.0B. Peer-median analysis across the five same-stage robotics comparables yields a median valuation of $500M, with a range of $300M to $1.8B. The peer universe exhibits substantial dispersion, reflecting stage-specific uncertainty in commercial traction and technology differentiation.

Triangulation across these methods supports a broad range of $425M to $21.9B, with a median estimate of $9.8B. The wide dispersion reflects fundamental tension between the current valuation's elevated positioning relative to peer medians and stage-progression expectations anchored to existing post-money. Key uncertainties include revenue run rate relative to peers, capital efficiency given burn profile, and near-term path to Series C financing. The current $10.0B valuation sits above the peer median but substantially below stage-progression upside, suggesting moderate premium pricing within the cohort.
